currentFocus: Static RE/Ghidra closure remains unchanged; public-safe rebuild support advanced only by materializing the tmm-arm4-readiness-gate proof-plan/checker slot, while PatchBench/Lore/WinUI integration preserved runtime, rebuild, storage, and payload non-claims.
publicPrimaryBoundary: The public repo may track RE docs, wave notes, scratch text exports, function indexes, static maps, proof summaries, checkers, and state batons. Hard payloads, full Ghidra databases/backups, raw captures, copied executables, secrets, build output, package output, local proof paths, raw manifests, and local campaign/worktree paths remain out of tracked state.

rebuildReadiness.sourceBoundary: The checker consumes only tracked public-safe files and validates source continuity, expected counters, front-door links, fail-closed public-boundary wording, and non-claims.
rebuildReadiness.futureGate: A later readiness-gate proof needs a separate bounded assignment and must not reuse this checker as a generic gate without updating the bound source proof and expected counters.

patchBenchProjectionBoundary.status: pre-extraction behavior tests/scaffold integrated; no RE/runtime behavior changed
patchBenchProjectionBoundary.summary: PatchBench receipt behavior tests and an unwired primitive formatter scaffold are integrated. This does not alter RE state, runtime proof, Ghidra storage, AppCore receipt construction, launch behavior, or patch/catalog semantics.
patchBenchProjectionBoundary.futureGate: Before helper wiring, add concatenated/interpolated sentinel tests, explicit manifest filename policy, Host/Join variant coverage, and page-versus-helper parity tests.

lorePackBoundary.summary: Lore pack relativePath index/content equality now fails closed in builder/check tooling. AppCore content-pack loading validates loaded document paths, duplicate ids/paths, hash mismatch, stale pack retention, and generic row-controlled error messages.
lorePackBoundary.futureGate: ZIP package probe parity for the same relativePath grammar remains open, along with a future decision on whether AppCore should also enforce index/content relativePath equality.

storageRetention.futureReviewBands: Medium and low values are non-authorized logical review ceilings only, not reclaimable storage, candidate pools, unique physical-size estimates, urgency signals, or deletion budgets.
storageRetention.futureMaintenance: Only future read-only manifest/hash/reference work is safe until a separate task grants explicit deletion authority and candidate-specific evidence.

runtimeProofBoundary.musicStatus: Music live-bundle readiness remains fail-closed and private pre-arm-only. Producer coverage or tooling availability is not live authority and not audible-output proof.
runtimeProofBoundary.onlineMultiplayer: not player-ready; Host/Join remains disabled until required distinct-endpoint command-source proof and source-bound copied-runtime causality proof are both accepted
runtimeProofBoundary.staticReClosureBoundary: Static closure is not runtime, gameplay, visual, online, rebuild, or no-noticeable-difference proof.

validation.stateRefresh: Integration state refresh only; no Ghidra mutation, runtime proof, generated rebuild code, hard payload, release publication, installed-game mutation, or original-BEA mutation.
validation.currentGateExpectation: Use JSON parse, stale local path/campaign marker scans, git diff --check, focused PatchBench/Lore/RE checks, docs/repo safety gates, public payload checks, and winui-primary-lane disposition.

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

nextSteps: Keep static RE mutation paused unless a product/runtime contradiction points back to a specific RE issue.
Treat tmm-arm4-readiness-gate as proof-plan/checker support only; do not claim readiness-gate execution, runtime proof, or rebuild parity.
Use the primitive projection contract and PatchBench receipt behavior tests as pre-extraction boundaries only; do not claim live receipt rendering safety until future wiring/parity tests exist.
Do not run live BEA/CDB/audio proof without explicit runtime-proof authority, leases, private proof-root readiness, arm phrase handling, and cleanup validation.
Do not delete Ghidra/proof material without a future candidate-specific manifest, retained alternative, dry-run, consults, authority, and acceptance.
